subject: Biology
title: The World's Oldest Trees
description: One-minute skill: The World's Oldest Trees.

hook.text: A bristlecone pine named Methuselah has been alive for over 4,850 years—older than the pyramids.

buildup.text: These trees grow in harsh mountain conditions where thin air and poor soil slow competition.

discovery.text: Their dense, resinous wood resists rot, insects, and fungi that kill younger trees.

twist.text: An even older tree was cut down in 1964 by a researcher who didn't realize its age until too late.

climax.text: Ancient trees are living climate archives. Their rings record droughts, fires, and volcanic eruptions.

punchline.text: These trees were seedlings when writing was invented.

quiz.question: How old is the bristlecone pine named Methuselah?
